teas.Compost Tea: Broad-spectrum microbial inoculant. Builds beneficial soil biology and improves nutrient uptake.
teas.Nettle Tea: High nitrogen boost. Stimulates leafy growth, strengthens stems, and repels pests.
teas.Banana Peel Tea: Rich in potassium and phosphorus. Promotes flowering, fruit development, and root strength.
teas.Moringa Tea: Natural growth stimulant containing cytokinins. Increases yield and improves stress resistance.
teas.Fish Emulsion: Fast-acting nitrogen and trace minerals. Excellent for vegetative growth and root development.

_meta.yeast_preparation: Mix 5g active dry yeast with 1L warm water (not hot) plus 1 tablespoon molasses or sugar. Stir well and let ferment 2-4 hours at room temperature until bubbly. Use same day.
_meta.yeast_dosing: Use yeast solution at 1:200 dilution — 5ml per 1 litre of water as soil drench. Apply monthly to introduce beneficial microorganisms. Do not apply as foliar.
_meta.dilution_note: Final dilution = ratio of your concentrate to total water volume. Example: 1:50 means add 10ml concentrate to 490ml water = 500ml total solution. Always add concentrate to water, not water to concentrate.
_meta.thyme_oil_mosquito_control: Add 5ml food-grade thyme oil to your spray mix. Apply around plant bases and surrounding soil at dusk for effective mosquito control. Safe for plants and edible crops when properly diluted.
application_tips: Apply foliar sprays early morning (before 9am) or late evening (after 5pm) to avoid leaf burn from UV.
Never mix different teas in the same application — apply each separately with minimum 24 hour gap.
Always water soil thoroughly before applying any drench fertilizer to prevent root burn.
Check leaf colour: yellow leaves = nitrogen deficiency; purple tinge = phosphorus deficiency; brown leaf edges = potassium deficiency.
Stop all foliar feeding at least 2 weeks before harvest to avoid residue on edible parts.
Rotate between different products each application to prevent microbial resistance and ensure balanced nutrition.
Keep detailed records of application dates, dilutions, and plant responses to optimise your schedule.

foliar.mixing_example: 10ml Compost Tea in 490ml water = 500ml foliar spray
foliar.notes: Keep dilution light to avoid burning young seedlings

foliar.mixing_example: 15ml Nettle Tea in 485ml water = 500ml foliar spray

drench.mixing_example: 10ml Compost Tea + 5ml Fish Emulsion in 485ml water

foliar.mixing_example: 25ml Banana Peel Tea + 12ml Moringa Tea in 463ml water
30ml Banana Peel Tea + 8ml Fish Emulsion in 462ml water

drench.mixing_example: 12ml each Banana Peel + Compost Tea in 476ml water
